name: sf-wrap
description: Binary-assertion test suite for /ren:wrap. Each test provides a fixture session transcript + expected classifier label + expected wiki-page diff plan. Tests are run by /ren:improve-skill's Karpathy loop (per ADR-012) and by CI. Solo-first (ADR-031): the default classifier is a conservative deterministic heuristic and there is no Activity Feed write.
_status: scaffold — fixtures pending; assertions are the testable goals as /ren:wrap implementation lands. Schema conforms to ADR-011 §eval.json schema.

tests.expected_output_summary: Classifier returns 'none'; zero wiki edits except CONTEXT.md rewrite + project log.md append.
binary_assertions: Classifier output 'candidate_artifacts' is empty (none implies empty)
No new file is created under wiki/projects/<active>/decisions/ after the run
No new file is created under wiki/projects/<active>/patterns/ after the run
wiki/projects/<active>/CONTEXT.md content differs from its pre-wrap state
wiki/projects/<active>/log.md has exactly one new line whose date matches today
Master wiki/log.md is byte-identical to its pre-wrap state (routine sessions skip master log)

tests.expected_output_summary: Deterministic classifier fires 'decision' on a deliberate phrase; new file under decisions/; STATE.md updated; master log appended.
binary_assertions: A new file is created at wiki/projects/<active>/decisions/<slug>.md where <slug> is non-empty kebab-case
The new decisions file's YAML frontmatter includes 'framework_version', 'schema_version', and 'type: decision' fields
wiki/projects/<active>/STATE.md contains a new entry under the 'Recent decisions' section pointing at the new decisions file
Master wiki/log.md gains exactly one new entry whose date matches today (non-routine sessions update master log)

tests.expected_output_summary: Session log is routine, but a /ren:note pin records a decision; the deterministic classifier escalates (pins dominate) and creates a decisions page.
binary_assertions: Classifier output 'labels' field contains 'decision' (escalated from the pinned note, not the routine log)
A new file is created under wiki/projects/<active>/decisions/ after the run
Master wiki/log.md gains exactly one new entry (a non-none signal fired)

tests.expected_output_summary: Mid-batch wiki write fails (simulated permission error); all wiki changes rolled back via git restore; user sees retry instructions.
binary_assertions: The command 'git restore wiki/' (or equivalent) was invoked during the run
After the run, wiki/ has zero diffs against its pre-run state (full rollback confirmed)
The WrapResult surfaces a non-empty apply_error explaining which diff failed
The user-facing output contains instructions explaining how to retry the wrap

non_triggers.prompt: Pin this for later: we decided to use Postgres.
What did we decide about auth last week?

_notes: Fixtures under fixtures/ are populated incrementally as /ren:wrap implementation lands.
Run via: uv run pytest skills/wrap/eval/ (TBD wiring).
These assertions are also the targets for /ren:improve-skill's Karpathy loop per ADR-012.
